sections.paragraph:
ποιμενικός, ή, όν (ποιμήν; Pla. et al.; poets since Theocr. 1, 23. In prose: Maximus Tyr. 20, 6b; 38, 2a; Philostrat., Imag. 2, 18 p. 370, 9; LXX; Philo; Jos., Ant. 6, 185) pertaining to a shepherd (Vi. Aesopi W 75 P. τὸ ποιμενικὸν σχῆμα) σχήματι ποιμενικῷ in the garb of a shepherd Hv 5:1.—DELG s.v. ποιμήν.

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Theocr Theocr , IV–III b.c.—List 5

Maximus Tyr Maximus Tyr , II a.d.—List 5

Philostrat Philostrat = Flavius Philostratus, the Sophist II–III a.d.—List 5

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Vi. Aesopi Vi. Aesopi , = Vitae Aesopi, I a.d.—List 5

Hv Hv = Visions

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 842
